title: thlaspi rotundifolium subsp. grignense
intro: Thlaspi rotundifolium subsp. grignense, also known as Alpine penny cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as supporting the immune system. Alpine penny cress can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish for various dishes.
